instrument.model: Resistive Hc2(T) transition + WHH extrapolation (review-paper compilation across multiple instruments)

sample_provenance.batch_lot: Godeke 2006 review compilation of A15 Nb3Sn polycrystalline wires from multiple manufacturers (OST/IGC/EAS/Furukawa) and single crystals; representative binary composition near stoichiometric Nb3Sn.

provenance.source_citation: A. Godeke, 'A Review of the Properties of Nb3Sn and Their Variation with A15 Composition, Morphology and Strain State', Superconductor Science and Technology 19, R68 (2006), arXiv:cond-mat/0606303. Reports Tc up to 18.3 K and Hc2(4.2 K) ~ 27-30 T for stoichiometric binary Nb3Sn (higher for ternary Ta- or Ti-doped variants).

scope_caveats: Digitized from published Hc2(T) compilation figure (Godeke 2006 SuST 19 R68 Fig. 16 / Fig. 18 family); values ±5%.
Hc2(0) = 30 T is the WHH extrapolation; the directly-measured Hc2(4.2 K) = 27 T is the headline value (less extrapolation-dependent).

Composition matters: binary Nb3Sn near stoichiometry yields the cited values; off-stoichiometric (Sn-deficient) bronze-route wires read Hc2(4.2 K) ~ 22-24 T. Ta- or Ti-doped ternary alloys reach 28-29 T at 4.2 K.
Strain state matters: zero-strain values cited; intrinsic Nb3Sn loses ~30% of Hc2 at 0.7% applied compressive strain (relevant for high-field magnet conductors).
Review-paper compilation across multiple wire vendors (OST/IGC/EAS/Furukawa); not a single-sample lot-test. replication_count reflects multi-decade A15 reproduction across labs.
citations: T. P. Orlando, E. J. McNiff, S. Foner, M. R. Beasley, PRB 19, 4545 (1979) — original Hc2(T) WHH analysis of Nb3Sn (Tinkham §4.10 reference)
